regime_justification: BOJ hiked 25bp to 1.0% yesterday as expected but USDJPY remains pinned at 160.38; spread still wide at ~180bp; JGBs rose post-hike signaling mixed confidence; intervention risk elevated but not firing.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY hovering at 160.38, just below the historical MOF intervention threshold of ~160; BOJ delivered widely-telegraphed hike to 1% yet yen remains weak; risk reversals data unavailable but vol subdued at ~7% implies complacency; carry still attractive at ~180bp spread.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY at 160 threshold, BOJ just hiked but no follow-through yen strength; oil collapsing (-27% monthly) eases import inflation pressure; VIX 16, MOVE 69 signal calm; intervention risk live but not imminent.

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: Next BOJ meeting July 30-31 (6 weeks out); US-Iran deal signing Friday could lock in lower oil, reducing inflation urgency; if USDJPY breaks above 161 MOF may act; otherwise carry grinds on.

3w.label: Unlikely
3w.driver: Oil peace dividend widening; if WTI holds <$80 import inflation abates and political pressure on MOF/BOJ eases; spread compression requires Fed cuts (not priced until Dec at 56%) or sharper BOJ tightening (July earliest).

4w.label: Unlikely
4w.driver: Month-end window; absent external shock or sharp USDJPY spike above 162, carry steady-state likely persists through July; macro vol (VIX, MOVE) at multi-month lows; crypto bid suggests risk-on intact.

bottom_line: BOJ delivered the expected 25bp hike to 1.0% on June 16 but USDJPY remains stubbornly at 160.38, just shy of intervention redlines. The yen ignored the tightening—spread still ~180bp, carry-to-vol ~26 (elevated), and vol at 7% signals complacency. Oil collapsing on US-Iran deal reduces imported-inflation urgency, lowering the odds MOF intervenes near-term unless USDJPY breaks 161+. Stage 2: pre-intervention watch. Grades stable at C/C/D/D; no unwind imminent, but setup fragile. If oil holds low and Fed stays put (Dec hike 56% priced), carry grinds higher into July BOJ meeting.

stack.note: Flat; 18 pips below 160.60, the April 2024 intervention level. BOJ hike yesterday had zero follow-through.
Subdued; well below April peak of ~14%. Market pricing zero intervention risk despite proximity to 160.
Fell to 3-week low on Iran deal; US-Iran peace easing inflation expectations, traders cut Fed hike odds.
Rebounded from 1-month low post-BOJ hike; dissent on board (Asada opposed) signals caution on further tightening.
Compressed modestly as US yields fell faster than JGBs rose; still elevated, carry attractive. Spread/vol ~26.
Flat; low-teens zone = complacency. Equity vol shows no stress despite geopolitical headlines.
Treasury vol at lows; <80 = calm bond market. No rates-volatility stress, consistent with dovish Fed pivot.
5-session slide arrested; -27% monthly on US-Iran deal. ¥/bbl = ¥12,229 (down sharply), import inflation easing.
Holding $60k floor; off Oct '25 ATH of $126k but stable. Risk-on bid intact, no crypto liquidity drain.
Hit fresh record after BOJ hike; equities unfazed by tightening, supported by weak yen export tailwind.

trigger_map.note: USDJPY at 160.38, just 18 pips shy of prior intervention threshold (~160.60 in Apr '24). MOF rhetoric quiet but Minister Katayama said 'deeply concerned' last week. Next catalyst: break above 161 or sharp 1-day spike.
25bp hike to 1.0% delivered Jun 16 as expected. Next meeting July 30-31; no hawkish acceleration signaled (one dissent). Next hike likely Sept/Oct earliest unless yen collapses further.
JGB at 2.64%, well below May peak of ~2.80%. Post-hike rebound modest; no breakout stress. Watch 2.80 level.
MOVE at 69, multi-month lows. Treasury vol dead calm. Iran deal = dovish Fed pivot, no rates volatility regime change imminent.
WTI collapsed -27% monthly to $76; US-Iran deal signed Friday eliminates Hormuz risk. ¥/bbl at ¥12,229, down from ~¥16k peak. Inflation tailwind for BOJ/MOF.
5-day change ~0% (flat to slightly down per sources). No disorderly pace; MOF historically reacts to velocity, not just level. Orderly drift = no urgency.

narrative.p1: Overnight: BOJ delivered the widely-expected 25bp hike to 1.0% on June 16, the first time the policy rate hit 1% since 1995. USDJPY barely flinched, closing at 160.38 today—essentially flat and just 18 pips below the historical MOF intervention threshold of ~160.60 set in April 2024. JGBs rallied modestly (10Y +4bp to 2.64%) but one board member (Asada) dissented, citing downside risks to growth. US 10Y fell 5bp to 4.44% on the US-Iran peace deal (signing Friday), which sent WTI down another leg in its 5-session slide (now -27% monthly). Nikkei hit a fresh record high, VIX and MOVE remain subdued. No intervention overnight; no sharp yen move. Stage unchanged from yesterday's brief: we remain at Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention).
narrative.p2: The quant anchors paint a fragile but stable picture. US-JP 10Y spread compressed 9bp to 180bps as US yields fell faster than JGBs rose; still wide and carry-positive. Carry-to-vol = 180/7 ≈ 26, elevated and signaling complacency (low vol despite high spread). USDJPY 5-day pace ~0%, orderly—MOF reacts to disorderly moves, not drift. Distance from 60d mean unavailable but price action shows consolidation at 160 rather than explosive breakout. WTI at $76 × 160.38 = ¥12,229/bbl, down sharply from the May peak of ~¥16k when WTI was near $110. That's the key deflationary input: oil collapse takes pressure off BOJ to hike aggressively and reduces political urgency for MOF intervention. VIX 16, MOVE 69—both at multi-month lows, signaling macro calm and low cross-asset stress. 1-week grade C: intervention risk elevated but not imminent; BOJ hiked but yen ignored it. 2-week grade C: July 30-31 BOJ meeting is the next catalyst; if USDJPY breaks 161+ before then, MOF may act. Otherwise, oil tailwind and subdued vol keep the setup in watch mode. I applied the unwind prior as a tiebreaker for week 2; absent the prior I'd lean D, but the proximity to 160 and the BOJ hike non-reaction justify holding at C.
narrative.p3: BTC at $65,794 is down 1.5% on the day but holding the $60k floor established in May. Off the October 2025 ATH of $126k by 48%, but the 7-day trend is +6.4% and the market cap remains above $1.3T. XRP and altcoin data unavailable, but BTC stability at $65k signals risk-on appetite is intact—no liquidity drain, no deleveraging cascade. Open interest direction and BTC-DXY correlation data not retrieved, but the fact that BTC is holding steady while USDJPY grinds at 160 and equity vol is low suggests crypto is tracking the 'carry-on' regime: spreads wide, funding easy, no forced unwind. For positioning: BTC's resilience is a positive carry-sympathy signal. If yen carry does unwind (Stage 3+), expect BTC to break $60k and test the low-$50k range as levered macro positions liquidate. Until then, crypto is a risk-on proxy, not a stress signal.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: Week 1 (C) reflects the immediate post-BOJ-hike environment: USDJPY at 160, intervention threshold proximity live, but no disorderly move and oil collapsing reduces urgency. Week 2 (C) holds because the July 30-31 BOJ meeting is the next scheduled catalyst, but it's 6 weeks out; nearer-term risk is a USDJPY breakout above 161 that forces MOF's hand. Applied unwind prior to hold at C; without it I'd lean D for week 2 given the oil tailwind.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: Week 2 (C) to week 3 (D): The US-Iran deal signing Friday (June 20) is expected to lock in lower oil prices and reopen Hormuz, cementing the disinflationary shift. By week 3, if WTI holds below $80 and USDJPY remains below 161, intervention risk recedes meaningfully. Fed hike odds for December are only 56%, so no near-term spread compression from that side. BOJ's next hike is July 30-31 earliest, likely later. Absent a shock, the setup drifts into a benign carry grind.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: Week 3 (D) to week 4 (D): Month-end flows and positioning adjustments are the main risk, but with VIX 16, MOVE 69, and oil collapsing, there's no catalyst for a forced unwind. If USDJPY breaks above 162 or a new exogenous shock emerges (geopolitical, Fed hawkish surprise), grades would flip back to C. Otherwise, carry steady-state persists into late July. Crypto bid and Nikkei at records support the 'risk-on intact' view.

watch_list: USDJPY daily close above 161.00—would trigger MOF rate-checks and verbal intervention, likely precursor to action within 24-48h.
US-Iran deal signing Friday June 20—confirmation that Hormuz reopens and oil supply normalizes; failure = oil spike, yen pressure resumes.
JGB 10Y break above 2.80%—would signal BOJ tightening expectations accelerating; supportive of yen, compresses carry.
Fed June 17-18 FOMC statement (today/tomorrow)—any hawkish surprise or pushback on rate-cut timeline would widen spread and pressure yen further.
USDJPY 1M implied vol spike above 10%—would indicate options market pricing intervention or shock; current 7% is complacent.
